Super Bear Adventure Mod Menu Guide - All Features & How to Use

Complete guide to the Super Bear Adventure mod menu. Learn every mod menu feature, how to activate options, best settings, and pro tips for gameplay.

June 13, 2026 Updated Jun 17, 2026 8 min read
Share:

What Is the Super Bear Adventure Mod Menu?

The mod menu is a powerful in-game overlay included with the Super Bear Adventure Mod APK that gives you access to a wide range of gameplay modifications. Think of it as a control panel that lets you customize your gameplay experience in real time, toggling features on and off as you see fit.

Unlike the standard game where you must work within the intended design constraints, the mod menu puts you in complete control. Want to explore a dangerous area without worrying about enemies? Toggle on God Mode. Need to reach a distant platform? Increase your jump height. Curious about what is behind a wall? Enable No Clip and walk right through it.

This guide covers every feature available in the mod menu, explains how to use each one effectively, and provides tips for getting the most out of your modded experience. If you have not installed the mod yet, visit our main download page for the latest version and installation instructions.

How to Access the Mod Menu

Opening the Menu

The mod menu is accessed through a floating button that appears on your screen during gameplay. Here is how to find and use it:

  1. Launch the game after installing the mod APK
  2. Look for the floating icon in the corner of your screen. It is typically a small circular button that can be dragged to any position
  3. Tap the floating icon to open the mod menu overlay
  4. Browse through the options using the scrollable menu interface
  5. Toggle features on or off by tapping the corresponding switches
  6. Close the menu by tapping the floating icon again or tapping outside the menu area

Repositioning the Menu Button

The floating menu button can be moved to any position on your screen:

  • Long press the floating icon
  • Drag it to your preferred location
  • Release to set its new position

This is useful for placing the button where it does not interfere with gameplay controls but remains easily accessible when you need it.

Menu Interface Layout

The mod menu is organized into categories for easy navigation:

  • Player Modifications: Features that affect Baaren’s abilities
  • World Modifications: Features that affect the game environment
  • Combat Modifications: Features that affect fighting and enemies
  • Visual Modifications: Features that change visual elements
  • Utility Features: Convenience tools and shortcuts

Player Modification Features

God Mode (Invincibility)

What it does: Makes Baaren completely immune to all damage sources, including enemy attacks, environmental hazards, fall damage, and boss attacks.

How to use it effectively:

  • Toggle on when exploring dangerous areas for the first time to learn layouts without frustration
  • Perfect for practicing boss fight patterns without the risk of dying
  • Useful when exploring secret areas that are heavily guarded
  • Ideal for completionists who want to focus on finding stickers without combat interruptions

Tips:

  • God Mode does not prevent knockback effects, so you can still be pushed off platforms by strong attacks
  • Consider disabling God Mode for boss fights once you have learned the patterns, as defeating bosses legitimately is more satisfying
  • In multiplayer, using God Mode may diminish the cooperative experience for other players

Speed Modification

What it does: Allows you to adjust Baaren’s movement speed with a slider ranging from 0.5x (half speed) to 5x (five times normal speed).

Speed settings and their uses:

Speed SettingBest Use Case
0.5xPrecise platforming, detailed exploration
1.0xNormal gameplay (default)
1.5xGeneral exploration, moderate traversal boost
2.0xQuick backtracking through known areas
3.0xSpeedrunning practice, long-distance travel
5.0xRapid world traversal, testing routes

Tips:

  • Higher speeds make precise platforming more difficult. Reduce speed when approaching tricky jump sequences
  • Speed modification stacks with ice physics in Snow Valley, which can make control challenging at high speeds
  • Using moderate speed boosts (1.5x-2x) is often more practical than maximum speed since it improves travel time without sacrificing too much control
  • Great for time trial medal practice by learning optimal routes at normal speed, then testing whether your route can meet gold requirements

Jump Enhancement

What it does: Modifies Baaren’s jumping capabilities with several sub-options:

  • Jump Height Multiplier: Increases the height of each jump (1x to 10x)
  • Infinite Double Jump: Allows unlimited mid-air jumps instead of just one double jump
  • Float Mode: Hold the jump button to float slowly downward after jumping

How to use it effectively:

  • Infinite double jump is incredibly useful for reaching secret areas and exploring vertical spaces
  • Moderate jump height increases (2x-3x) help with platforming without being too extreme
  • Float mode is perfect for precision landings on small platforms
  • Combine with speed modification for efficient aerial exploration of large worlds

Tips:

  • Extremely high jump height (5x+) can launch you out of the playable area. Use with caution
  • Infinite double jump trivializes most platforming challenges, so consider using it only for exploration rather than regular gameplay
  • Float mode works well in the Giant House where falling distances are significant

No Clip (Walk Through Walls)

What it does: Disables collision detection with walls, floors, and obstacles, allowing Baaren to pass through any solid object.

How to use it effectively:

  • Explore areas behind walls and under floors to find hidden content
  • Skip locked doors or barriers that normally require puzzle solutions
  • Move through the ground to discover underground areas or hidden rooms
  • Access developer areas or out-of-bounds regions (if any exist)

Tips:

  • Always note your position before using No Clip, as you can easily become disoriented when passing through solid objects
  • Disable No Clip before the ground beneath you disappears, or you may fall indefinitely
  • No Clip can reveal how game worlds are constructed, which some players find interesting from a game design perspective
  • Be careful using this in the Mole Mines as underground collision geometry can be complex

Teleportation

What it does: Allows instant travel to specific locations marked on the map or to coordinates you set manually.

Teleportation modes:

  • Map Teleport: Open the world map, tap any location, and instantly travel there
  • Bookmark Teleport: Save up to 10 custom locations and teleport to them at any time
  • NPC Teleport: Instantly travel to the location of any known NPC
  • Spawn Teleport: Return to the world’s spawn point instantly

Tips:

  • Bookmark useful locations like shopkeepers, quest NPCs, and secret area entrances
  • Map Teleport works across different world sections, making it faster than running
  • NPC Teleport is helpful when you need to turn in quests or buy items
  • Combined with unlimited money, teleportation to shops lets you quickly purchase and try new cosmetics

Combat Modification Features

One-Hit Kill

What it does: Every attack from Baaren defeats any enemy or boss in a single hit, regardless of the target’s normal health pool.

How to use it effectively:

  • Clear enemy-heavy areas quickly when your goal is exploration rather than combat
  • Useful for farming enemy-dropped items without time-consuming fights
  • Speeds up mission completion when combat segments are repetitive
  • Can be combined with God Mode for completely stress-free gameplay

Tips:

  • Using One-Hit Kill on bosses removes the challenge and spectacle of multi-phase boss fights, so consider saving it for repeat encounters rather than first-time boss meetings
  • Some medal challenges specifically require defeating enemies under certain conditions, and One-Hit Kill may interfere with these

Freeze Enemies

What it does: All enemies in the current area stop moving and cannot attack. They become stationary obstacles that can be defeated normally or simply ignored.

How to use it effectively:

  • Create a safe environment for thorough exploration
  • Study enemy positions and patrol routes before engaging them normally
  • Take screenshots of enemy placements for guide creation
  • Practice platforming sections near enemies without combat pressure

Tips:

  • Frozen enemies still have their collision boxes, so they can block paths
  • Unfreezing enemies returns them to their normal behavior immediately
  • Boss enemies may require a brief moment to re-sync their animations when unfrozen

Unlimited Stamina

What it does: Prevents stamina or energy from depleting, allowing unlimited use of stamina-based actions like sprinting, special attacks, and sustained abilities.

Tips:

  • This feature is most noticeable in areas where extended sprinting is needed
  • Combine with speed modification for maximum traversal efficiency
  • Useful in timed challenges where stamina management would normally slow you down

World Modification Features

Auto-Collect

What it does: Automatically collects coins, items, and pickups within a configurable radius around Baaren. The collection radius can be set from small (nearby items only) to large (items across a wide area).

Radius settings:

  • Small: Collects items within arm’s reach (slight convenience boost)
  • Medium: Collects items within a moderate area (good for general play)
  • Large: Collects items within a wide radius (efficient for coin farming)
  • Maximum: Collects all items in the current area regardless of distance

Tips:

  • Combined with unlimited money, auto-collect mostly serves as a convenience feature since coins are already infinite
  • Auto-collect picks up stickers and special items too, which is helpful for sticker hunting
  • Maximum radius can cause performance issues on older devices since it processes many items simultaneously
  • Does not automatically collect items that require specific interaction (like quest items that need a button press)

Weather and Time Control

What it does: Allows you to change the current weather conditions and time of day in any world.

Options:

  • Clear skies, rain, snow, fog, or storm weather
  • Dawn, morning, noon, afternoon, sunset, or night time settings
  • Static time (prevent time of day from changing)

Tips:

  • Changing weather can reveal hidden items that only appear in specific conditions
  • Night mode creates atmospheric exploration experiences
  • Clear skies at noon provides the best visibility for finding hidden objects
  • Snow weather in non-snow worlds creates amusing visual combinations

Visual Modification Features

Camera Options

What it does: Provides advanced camera controls beyond the standard game settings:

  • Zoom Range: Expand the zoom-in and zoom-out range significantly
  • Free Camera: Detach the camera from Baaren to explore the world independently
  • First Person Mode: Switch to a first-person perspective
  • Cinematic Mode: Widen the field of view for a more cinematic appearance

Tips:

  • Free camera is excellent for scouting ahead in dangerous areas
  • First-person mode makes the game feel completely different and is worth trying
  • Extreme zoom-out reveals the full layout of game areas, useful for navigation planning
  • Cinematic mode looks great for screenshots but reduces peripheral awareness

Visual Filters

What it does: Apply visual filters to change the game’s appearance:

  • Retro pixel filter
  • Noir black-and-white filter
  • Vibrant color boost
  • Pastel softening filter
  • Night vision green filter

Tips:

  • Filters are purely cosmetic and do not affect gameplay
  • Night vision is useful when exploring dark areas like caves in the Mole Mines
  • The noir filter creates a unique atmosphere for screenshots

Utility Features

Screenshot Mode

What it does: Hides all UI elements and the mod menu overlay for clean screenshots.

Tips:

  • Combine with camera options and visual filters for unique screenshots
  • Great for capturing the game’s beautiful environments without clutter
  • Use the Freeze Enemies feature to set up perfect action shots

Performance Monitor

What it does: Displays real-time performance information including FPS, memory usage, and network latency.

Tips:

  • Useful for identifying performance issues in specific areas
  • Check our lag fix guide if you notice performance drops
  • Network latency display is helpful for diagnosing multiplayer lag

Best Mod Menu Configurations

For First-Time Players

If you are new to the game and want a gentle introduction:

  • God Mode: ON (explore without fear)
  • Speed: 1.0x (normal, learn the natural pace)
  • Jump: 1.5x height (slight assistance without breaking platforming)
  • Auto-Collect: Medium radius (convenience without trivializing exploration)
  • Other features: OFF (experience the game naturally first)

Read our beginner guide alongside these settings for the best start.

For Completionists

If you are hunting for 100% completion:

  • God Mode: OFF (earn achievements legitimately)
  • Speed: 1.5x (save time between objectives)
  • No Clip: OFF by default, toggle ON only for investigating potential secret locations
  • Auto-Collect: Large radius (never miss a collectible)
  • Teleportation: Active with bookmarks at key locations

For Exploration Enthusiasts

If you want to discover every hidden corner:

  • God Mode: ON (explore dangerous areas freely)
  • No Clip: Available for investigating suspicious walls
  • Jump: Infinite double jump (reach any height)
  • Camera: Free camera available for scouting
  • Speed: 2.0x (cover more ground efficiently)

For Speedrunners

If you are practicing speed routes:

  • Speed: Variable (match target speed for practice)
  • Teleportation: Bookmarks at route start points
  • Performance Monitor: ON (track frame rate consistency)
  • God Mode: OFF (practice under real conditions)
  • Timer features: Active for route timing

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I use the mod menu in multiplayer?

The mod menu technically works in multiplayer sessions, but we recommend disabling most features when playing with others. Features like God Mode and One-Hit Kill create an unfair advantage and can diminish the experience for other players.

Will using the mod menu affect my game saves?

The mod menu modifications are applied in real time and are not saved to your game file. Your save data reflects your actual in-game progress (coins collected, areas explored, missions completed). Toggling mod features does not corrupt or alter save files.

Can I earn medals with the mod menu active?

You can attempt medal challenges with the mod menu active, but certain mod features may prevent medals from registering. Combat medals that require no-damage completion will not count if God Mode is enabled, and time trial medals may not register if speed modification is active. For legitimate medal earning, disable all mod features.

Does the mod menu drain battery faster?

The mod menu overlay uses minimal additional resources. Features like auto-collect on maximum radius or performance monitor may increase CPU usage slightly, but the impact on battery life is negligible for most devices.

How do I update the mod menu?

The mod menu is included with the mod APK and is updated alongside it. When you download the latest mod APK version, it includes the latest mod menu with any new features or improvements. Check our version changelogs to see what has changed.

Can I customize the mod menu interface?

The mod menu supports basic customization:

  • Change the opacity of the menu overlay
  • Resize the floating button
  • Choose between light and dark menu themes
  • Reorder features within categories
  • Create quick-toggle shortcuts for frequently used features

Conclusion

The Super Bear Adventure mod menu transforms how you interact with the game, offering unprecedented control over your gameplay experience. Whether you use it as a gentle assist to make the game more accessible or as a full suite of tools for creative exploration, the mod menu enhances every aspect of your time in the Kingdom of Earthkwak.

Remember that the most enjoyable approach is often a balanced one. Use mod features to remove frustration, not to skip the fun parts. The game’s missions, boss fights, and secret discoveries are designed to be rewarding, and experiencing them with just enough mod assistance can create the perfect gameplay balance.

For the full mod experience including the menu and all other features, download the latest version from our main download page.
